mentions.note: Up 11% in 2025 despite awful EV core business (earnings down 60% from 2022). Market ignores fundamentals, focused on robo-taxis and humanoid robots in Austin. Musk showmanship drives sentiment. Needs progress on both robotics to keep bulls happy; stabilizing auto business would help but won't matter to some bulls.
Preferred play for robotics exposure; stock flat recently but robotics potential significant; time for Tesla to deliver on automation promise.
Part of Magnificent 7; experiencing P/E compression like peers but company too strong to bet against long-term; stay the course.
Repeat: Tesla is robot company and autonomous driving play, not just car company. This quarter could define new narrative — if so, stock skyrockets from earnings announcement.

Doesn't trade on EV fundamentals (business in bad shape); trades on Musk storytelling. Need clarity on self-driving taxis and humanoid robots (promised end-2027 but history of timeline misses). Report Wednesday.
Reports Wednesday but trades on narrative not numbers. EV business weak. Musk credibility on self-driving taxis and humanoid robots (2027 launch) paramount — needs concrete progress updates.
Beat EPS and revenue Q4; 1.4B free cash flow impressive; robo-taxi/robot intros ahead of expectations driving after-hours strength.
Reframed as robotics/cyber/humanoid robot juggernaut, not just car company; Cramer buyer; give me five robots.
No longer a car company; cyber-robot/XAI/SpaceX merger pending(?); hasn't blasted off. General Motors MORE magnificent; prefer it.
Beat numbers but EV is past; future on cybercabs and humanoid robots; stock down 3%; uncertain narrative; Musk acolytes more excited about SpaceX.
Beat numbers but EV focus irrelevant; future about cybercabs and robots—higher capex than expected; down 3%; Musk acolytes prefer SpaceX IPO.
Q1 deliveries miss, energy storage collapse, 60% earnings decline since 2022; scarcity value evaporates with SpaceX IPO; stock likely to drift lower until robotaxi/robot progress or auto turnaround.
Reports Wednesday close; market wants self-driving cars, robots, SpaceX IPO updates — not traditional autos; no interest in pigeonholing as auto company anymore.
Cramer endorsement; robotics and full self-driving represent future growth. Critics selling the future are wrong; same bears probably short Intel.
Magnificent Seven member (but Cramer's charitable trust held only 6 of 7, not Tesla initially); borrowed heavily for growth with massive opportunity.

Self-driving and robots positioned as growth engines; potential SpaceX merger discussed as option for Musk to escape noise. May struggle maintaining trillionaire status if capital pulled toward SpaceX/X.

Potential merger with SpaceX under Musk control ($1.5T+25% premium). Self-driving, battery, robot (humanoid) businesses; Starlink connectivity could enable distributed robot operations. Speculative but aligned with Musk's long-term vision.
